data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the sprint orchestrator to drive a PR-per-batch loop: after each batch, create a PR via the merge primitive (delivered by 8787-ac16), wait for CI green via `gh pr checks`, and only start the next batch once the PR merges. Epic completion is the final batch's PR merge — not a separate cumulative push.
**Why**: Once 8787-ac16 makes merge PR-based, sprint's existing "merge at epic close" model becomes incorrect. CI (including `validate.sh --ci` once 3aaa-0238's scope expansion lands) must be the inter-batch gate so regressions are caught between batches, not at epic close.
**Scope**:
- IN: sprint SKILL.md changes to invoke the PR primitive per batch, wait-on-CI loop between batches, removal of the cumulative epic-close push, handling of CI-red between batches (block next batch, surface failure), interaction with `--resume` so a sprint resuming mid-epic re-enters the per-batch loop correctly
- OUT: merge-to-main.sh / PR primitive itself (8787-ac16), local hook removal (5428-bebd), documentation updates (ed9c-a277), CI pipeline definition (3aaa-0238)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint creates a PR after each batch and waits for CI green before starting the next batch
  ← Satisfies SC9: "sprint orchestrator creates a PR per batch ... waits for CI green ... merges via gh pr merge after each batch"
- When this story is complete, epic completion in sprint is the final batch's PR merge — there is no separate cumulative push step
  ← Satisfies SC9: "epic completion is the final batch's PR merge — not a separate cumulative push"
- When this story is complete, a CI failure on a batch PR blocks the next batch from starting and surfaces the failure to the user with actionable context
- When this story is complete, sprint `--resume` re-enters the per-batch loop correctly (detects in-flight PR, resumes wait-on-CI rather than re-creating)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Interface dependency] Depends on 8787-ac16's merge primitive interface (PR create, wait, merge as a callable unit). Coordinate the contract before implementation.
- [Latency] Inter-batch CI latency bounds sprint cadence. Documented in epic 1083-fb3d Scenario Analysis as expected behavior, not a regression.
- [State] Sprint's existing batch state machine must be extended to track "PR open / CI pending / merged" between batches; resume must read this correctly.
- [Caller impact] end-session SKILL.md and any other caller assuming sprint pushes at epic close must be reviewed for compatibility.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As an agent, sprint creates a PR per batch and waits for CI before the next batch
